invariant: A single constrained transition lane governs system throughput, so upstream demand accumulates and downstream flow is paced by that narrow step.
joinPattern: inflow -> constrained review/approval/write lane -> queued backlog -> delayed downstream completion
failureSignature: Removing or parallelizing the suspected narrow lane does not materially reduce queue depth, cycle time, or throughput variance; flow remains constrained by another mechanism.
repairLogic: Identify the narrow lane, split or parallelize it, add bounded transition rules, and verify stabilization by before/after queue-depth and cycle-time measurements.
disconfirmationCondition: If controlled lane-splitting or reviewer/processor parallelization produces no meaningful improvement in throughput or queue behavior within the declared window, classify as not bottleneck (likely saturation, gate dependency, or mixed assembly).
exampleObservables: queue growth with one mandatory reviewer
single approval lane delays all downstream work
review backlog rises each cycle while inflow stays steady
single write shard queue spikes under load
release manager overload creates missed release windows
